title: Memory Foam vs Egg Crate Dog Beds – Which Is Actually Better?
seoTitle: Memory Foam vs Egg Crate Dog Beds – Honest Comparison (2026)
metaDescription: Memory foam vs egg crate foam for dog beds: we compare support, durability, cooling, and price. One clearly wins for dogs with joint issues.
author: GetPawsy Pet Care Team

sections.heading: The Core Difference
sections.content:
Memory foam (viscoelastic polyurethane) responds to body heat and pressure, conforming to your dog's unique shape. It distributes weight across the entire surface, eliminating pressure points. It springs back after each use.

Egg crate foam (convoluted polyurethane) has a textured surface that provides initial softness and airflow. However, it doesn't conform to body shape, doesn't distribute weight evenly, and compresses flat within 3–6 months.

For dogs with joint issues, the winner is clear: memory foam orthopedic beds provide genuine therapeutic support that egg crate simply cannot match.

sections.heading: Head-to-Head Comparison
sections.content:
Support: Memory foam ★★★★★ vs Egg Crate ★★☆☆☆. Memory foam molds to joints; egg crate just adds surface cushion.

Durability: Memory foam lasts 3–5 years; egg crate lasts 3–6 months before flattening.

Cooling: Egg crate wins here — the textured surface promotes airflow. Memory foam retains heat (unless gel-infused).

Price: Egg crate is 40–60% cheaper upfront, but costs more over time due to frequent replacement.

Weight capacity: Memory foam supports dogs 100+ lbs without bottoming out. Egg crate compresses under 40+ lbs.

See also: memory foam vs standard dog bed

sections.heading: When Egg Crate Foam Makes Sense
sections.content:
Egg crate foam isn't always wrong. It works for: puppies under 6 months (who'll outgrow the bed), temporary/travel beds, dogs under 20 lbs with no joint issues, and backup/secondary beds.

For any dog over 30 lbs, any senior dog, or any dog with joint symptoms, memory foam is the only responsible choice.

sections.heading: Our Recommendation
sections.content:
If your dog weighs over 30 lbs or is older than 5 years, invest in a high-density memory foam bed (1.8+ lb/ft³). The upfront cost is higher, but a single memory foam bed outlasts 6–10 egg crate beds.

Browse our curated orthopedic dog bed collection — every bed is tested for foam density and durability. For large breeds, see our best beds for large dogs.

faq.question: Is egg crate foam orthopedic?
faq.answer: No. Despite marketing claims, egg crate foam doesn't provide true orthopedic support. It adds surface cushion but doesn't conform to joints or distribute weight. Only viscoelastic memory foam qualifies as genuinely orthopedic.

faq.question: Can I put egg crate foam on top of memory foam?
faq.answer: You can, but it reduces the memory foam's ability to conform to your dog's body. The egg crate layer acts as a barrier between your dog and the therapeutic foam surface. It's better to use memory foam alone.
